frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Arre bhai, these days girls put so much makeup, then in real life they look totally different. Natural is always better, but at the same time thoda presentable toh rehna chahiye — if someone makes no effort at all, that also doesn't look good.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: condemning visible makeup as deceptive while also expecting them to maintain an attractive appearance. It encodes the 'natural beauty' ideal, deception framing, and appearance obligation.
original_frame: Women's Opinions Dismissed: She Doesn't Know Enough to Have a Say
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at a family dinner where the conversation has turned to politics Ñ the upcoming state elections, which party to support, what the issues are. Your wife makes a comment about the local candidate and why she thinks he is better than the alternative. She has a specific reason Ñ she has been following the water shortage issue in your area and thinks this candidate has a better plan. You are responding to her comment in front of your two adult sons and your mother. How you respond reveals whether her political opinion is treated as a contribution to the conversation or as something to be managed.
